info.title: Rolls API
info.description: OpenAPI spec for the Rolls Rails API (https://rolls.yannick.computer).

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile and registered WebAuthn credentials.
Returns whether an invite is needed (true when no users exist yet).

post.description: Checks whether a username is available. Optionally validates an invite code.
Returns WebAuthn credential creation options. The response includes all WebAuthn option fields at the top level plus a nested `options` object with the same fields, and a `challenge` string.
Verifies a WebAuthn registration response and creates a session (Set-Cookie). The credential can be sent either as flat fields at the top level or nested under a `response` key.

Returns WebAuthn authentication options. The response includes all WebAuthn option fields at the top level plus a nested `options` object, a `challenge` string, and optionally a `user_id` when an identifier is provided.

identifier.description: Username or email. Omit for discoverable credential flow.

post.description: Verifies a WebAuthn authentication response and creates a session (Set-Cookie). The credential can be sent either as flat fields at the top level or nested under a `response` key.
Returns options for a discoverable credential / autofill flow. No `allowCredentials` are set. Use the same login-verify endpoint to complete authentication.

get.description: Returns API keys for the authenticated user. Raw keys are never returned.

post.description: Creates a new API key. The `raw_key` is returned only once.

get.description: Creates a new API key labelled 'CLI'. If a `callback` query param is provided, redirects to `{callback}?key={raw_key}`. Otherwise returns `{ raw_key }`. Requires session cookie auth.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Redirect URL to append the generated key to

302.description: Redirect to callback URL with key

get.description: Returns invite codes created by the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new invite code. Admins can specify custom `max_uses` and `expires_at`; regular users get a single-use invite.

get.description: Checks whether an invite code is valid. No auth required.

post.description: Sign in or register using an Apple identity token. If the Apple ID is not linked to any account and no `username` is provided, returns 404 with `{ error: 'new_account_required', email }`. If `username` is provided, creates a new account.

full_name.description: Only sent by Apple on first sign-in

username.description: Required when creating a new account

get.description: Returns all rolls for the authenticated user, ordered by created_at desc. Supports pagination.
Returns the next auto-generated roll number in YYxNN format (e.g. `26x01`).
Returns all non-archived rolls with nested camera and film associations, ordered by created_at desc.
Returns all archived rolls with nested camera and film associations, ordered by archived_at desc.

post.description: Sets one field to one value across multiple rolls identified by roll_number.

patch.description: Partial update. All fields are optional.

get.description: Proxies the contact sheet image from R2 and returns it as `image/webp`.

put.description: Uploads raw image bytes to R2 and saves the resulting public URL on the roll. Request body is the raw file bytes.

get.description: Returns all cameras for the authenticated user, ordered by updated_at desc.

post.description: Slug is auto-generated from brand+model if not provided. Upserts on (user_id, slug). Returns 201 for new cameras, 200 for updated.
Reassigns all rolls from source cameras to the target camera, then deletes the source cameras.

source_ids.description: Source camera UUIDs to merge into target

delete.description: Returns 409 if the camera has rolls associated with it.

get.description: Returns all films for the authenticated user, ordered by updated_at desc.

post.description: Slug is auto-generated from brand+name if not provided. Upserts on (user_id, slug). Returns 201 for new films, 200 for updated.
Reassigns all rolls from source films to the target film, then deletes the source films.

source_ids.description: Source film UUIDs to merge into target

delete.description: Returns 409 if the film has rolls associated with it.

get.description: Returns the global film catalog. No authentication required.

post.description: Upserts cameras, films, and rolls. Cameras/films are keyed by slug; rolls are keyed by uuid then roll_number.

get.description: Returns all cameras, films, and rolls for the authenticated user.
Returns the latest `updated_at` timestamp for rolls, cameras, and films. Use to skip a full re-fetch when nothing has changed.

BearerAuth.description: Raw API key (`rk_*`) or JWT token

CookieAuth.name: session
CookieAuth.description: JWT session cookie set by login/register endpoints

User.description: Full user object returned by auth endpoints

WebAuthnOptionsResponse.description: WebAuthn options response. All standard WebAuthn option fields are returned at the top level AND nested under `options`. `challenge` is always present as a base64url string.

challenge.description: Base64url-encoded challenge to echo back in the verify request

options.description: Nested copy of the full WebAuthn options object

user_id.description: User UUID, present when an identifier was provided

WebAuthnVerifyBody.description: Credential can be sent as flat fields at the top level (if `id` is present) or nested under a `response` key.

id.description: Credential id — present when sending credential fields flat

response.description: Credential object — present when wrapping the credential

challenge.description: Challenge echoed from the options response (used by some clients)

user_id.description: User UUID echoed from the login options response

Roll.description: Film roll as returned by list/index endpoints (no nested associations).

shot_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime string (date stored as date column, serialized via .to_time.iso8601)

scanned_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime string (date stored as date column, serialized via .to_time.iso8601)

tags.description: Postgres text array; empty array if none

status.description: Derived from timestamps: archived > uploaded > processed > scanned > lab > fridge > loaded

RollWithAssociations.description: Roll with nested camera and film objects (returned by show, home, archive, create, update).

value.description: Value to set (string, number, null, or array for tags)

gradient_from.description: CSS color value for gradient start

gradient_to.description: CSS color value for gradient end

CatalogFilm.description: Global film catalog entry (no user_id, no uuid, keyed by slug).

slug.description: Slug key for upsert; auto-generated from brand+model if omitted
Slug key for upsert; auto-generated from brand+name if omitted

uuid.description: Primary upsert key; falls back to roll_number

rolls.description: All rolls ordered by created_at asc
